data.title: Diageo Seems to Be Managing What It Can Control — Market Talk
data.description: Diageo is increasingly in control of the elements of its business it can manage amid a tough environment for spirits makers, Citi analyst Simon Hales writes in a research note. The U.K. distiller reported solid results for its fiscal third quarter through March and unveiled a restructuring program, Hales says. The top-line outlook, however, remains unclear, he adds. Drinks makers are grappling with a slowdown in alcohol consumption, as well as the potential hit from U.S. tariffs. "We continue to believe in the attractive long-term fundamentals of the spirits industry and yet to be accessed 'hidden value' in Guinness," the analyst says. Citi reiterates its buy rating on the stock. Shares trade 0.9% lower. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

data.title: Íslandsbanki hf.: Upcoming covered bond auction 26 May
data.description:
Íslandsbanki will offer covered bonds in a new series, ISB CB 31, on Monday, 26 May 2025.

ISB CB 31 is a new bond with a fixed rate annual coupon. The final maturity date is 3 June 2031.

The bonds will be offered in a single-price auction, where all accepted offers will be awarded at the highest accepted yield. Íslandsbanki reserves the right to accept or reject offers in part or in whole.

Expected settlement date is 3. June 2025.

The covered bonds will be issued under the Bank’s covered bond programme. The Base Prospectus is available here: Covered bonds programme.

The auction will be managed by Íslandsbanki’s Fixed Income Sales. Offers shall be submitted by e-mail to vbm@isb.is before 16:00 p.m. on 26 May 2025.

data.title: Taiwan M2 Money Supply Growth Moderates in April
data.description:
The Broad M2 Money Supply in Taiwan rose by 3.88% year-on-year to TWD 65,003 billion in April 2025, easing from a 4.38% growth in March.

The deceleration was primarily driven by a slowdown in the growth of loans and investments.

For the January–April period, the average year-on-year growth stood at 4.74%.

data.title: Workday Is Maintained at Neutral by Piper Sandler
REG-The BANK of Greenland has issued DKK 40 million in Tier 2 capital
data.description:
The BANK of Greenland has issued DKK 40 million in Tier 2 capital

The BANK of Greenland has today agreed to issue DKK 40 million in Tier 2 capital as part of the ongoing process to optimize the capital structure.

The capital is issued with ISIN no. DK0030551403 and with effect from 28 May 2025. The Tier 2 capital has a maturity of ten years with a possibility to call after five years and any time thereafter subject to approval by the Danish Financial Supervisory Authority (Finanstilsynet). The bonds have a floating rate agreed at a six-month CIBOR rate plus 300 basis points.

Nykredit Bank A/S is the sole lead manager of the issue.

Best regards

The BANK of Greenland

Martin Kviesgaard

Managing Director

Contact: +299 34 78 02 / mail: mbk@banken.gl

Attachment

  • 07.Tier2-issuing_UK

data.title: Indonesia issues $720 million of Samurai bonds, mulls yuan and Australian dollar offers
data.description:
Indonesia issued on Friday 103.2 billion yen ($719.77 million) of samurai bonds of three- to 20-year maturities, its finance ministry said.

It sold three-year bonds with a 1.56% coupon, five-year bonds with a 1.87% coupon, seven-year bonds with a 2.05% coupon, 10-year bonds with a 2.35% coupon and 20-year bonds with a 3.26% coupon.

The ministry said the 20-year bond has been issued in form of "blue bonds", proceeds of which are used to focus ocean-related projects.

The ministry also said it was considering a plan to diversify its debt instruments by issuing "dim sum", or offshore yuan bonds, and "kangaroo" Australian dollar bonds.

($1 = 143.3800 yen)

data.title: Slovenia Tourist Arrivals Surge 22.5% in April
data.description:
Tourist arrivals in Slovenia surged by 22.5% year-on-year to 490,272 in April 2025, following a 9.5% fall in the previous month.

The number of foreign tourists grew by 31.1% to 379,333, while domestic arrivals edged up by 0.1% to 110,939.

Most foreign visitors came from Hungary (92.3%), Germany (43.8%), Austria (34.5%), Italy (26%), and Croatia (18.9%).

Meanwhile, overnight stays increased by 21.8% to 1,140,944 in April.

The highest number of tourist overnight stays was recorded in mountain resorts (37.4%), seaside resorts (21.6%), and the capital (26.2%).

data.title: Emerging market equity funds lure investors fleeing overvalued U.S. assets
data.description:
Emerging market equity funds are leading the global performance this year, bolstered by attractive valuations, years of under-positioning by investors and an easing of economic pressures after U.S. President Donald Trump's pause on tariffs.

According to data compiled by LSEG, funds tracking equities in Latin America and emerging Europe have each gained around 24% so far this year, while broader emerging market equity funds are up 9.3%.

Notably, equity funds focused on Morocco, Colombia, Greece, Brazil, and Portugal have each returned more than 30% this year. In comparison, U.S.-focused equity funds have returned just 0.17%, and global equity funds are up 6.8%.

The outperformance of emerging markets marks a reversal after their years of lagging developed markets, during which U.S. equities, driven by the AI-led tech rally, delivered stellar index gains.

This year, however, investors have been selling U.S. assets as worries over possible recession, fiscal instability and Trump's erratic policies shake their faith in the dollar.

LSEG Lipper data showed dedicated EM equity funds have attracted $10.6 billion in inflows in the first five months of the year, a 43% increase over the same period last year.

Malcolm Dorson, emerging markets senior portfolio manager at GlobalX, attributed this to how under-owned emerging market equities are. U.S. investors allocate just 3–5% to emerging markets, well below the 10.5% weighting in the MSCI Global Index and far short of their roughly 25% share of global market capitalisation, he said.

"Allocators are dangerously short a deeply discounted and fast-growing asset class," he said.

Analysts also highlight improving fundamentals. Latin American countries are largely insulated from tariffs, given their trade deficits with the U.S., while Asian economies are pivoting toward domestic consumption.

Further, J.P.Morgan upgraded its rating on emerging market stocks to "overweight" from "neutral" earlier this week. It said it expects all central banks across developing economies, excluding Brazil, to ease monetary policy, which could increase economic activity and the attractiveness of equity markets.

Gains in tech stocks have buoyed Chinese and Hong Kong equities, drawing renewed interest from foreign investors seeking to invest in artificial intelligence and other low-cost tech firms such as DeepSeek.

There are opportunities in Mexico and Brazil, which have remained resilient despite trade tensions, according to Alison Shimada, portfolio manager at Allspring Global Investments.

"The China consumer story is especially interesting right now," she said. "Beijing is very focused on stimulating the consumer economy. India may be overbought, but there are pockets of opportunity such as power companies and non-bank financials."

At the end of last month, the MSCI Emerging Markets Index (.dMIEF00000PUS) was trading at a forward 12-month price-to-earnings ratio (P/E) of 11.96, just below its 10-year average of 12.1.

In contrast, the MSCI USA (.dMIUS00000PUS) and MSCI World indexes (.MIWO00000PUS) were trading at 20.5 and 18.1, well above their 10-year averages of 18.8 and 16.9, respectively.

data.title: Polypropylene (PP) contract prices trend lower in Europe
data.description: This week, PP contract prices quoted lower in the European region. An industry source in Europe informed a Polymerupdate team member, "In the European polypropylene market, contract prices experienced a decline this week due to persistently weak demand exerting downward pressure on pricing. Contract negotiations were reported to have concluded with reductions mirroring those in the propylene feedstock. The polypropylene market remains oversupplied, with increased material availability stemming from backlogged stocks from April, contributing to a bearish sentiment and causing concern among sellers, which in turn has led to further price declines in May. Despite some logistical challenges and isolated plant outages, these factors have had minimal effect on alleviating the overall demand weakness from key downstream sectors such as construction, automotive, and packaging�elements that have consistently reinforced the prevailing bearish outlook in the European polypropylene landscape. A decrease of Euro 65/mt in the May monthly contract price (MCP) for feedstock propylene has established a bearish context for polypropylene prices this month, followed by heightened selling activity as distributors engaged in destocking. The European polypropylene market continues to be characterized by low demand, mirroring the situation in April. The automotive sector is experiencing soft orders, exacerbated by a downturn in European car production due to the 25% tariffs imposed by the US on auto-part imports. Demand for packaging remains stable to weak, particularly in the BOPP segment, although not all market participants report weak demand for flexible packaging. A slight uptick in demand for caps and closures has been noted, which aligns with the seasonal increase in beverage bottle production during the warmer summer months.The source added, "The cost of polypropylene (PP) has continued to face downward pressure due to a persistent reduction in production expenses. Following a decrease of EUR 55/tonne in April, the European propylene contract price fell by an additional EUR 65/tonne in May 2025. This extended decline in feedstock prices has diminished the pricing power of producers, significantly contributing to the decrease in PP prices. The ongoing surplus of Polypropylene (PP) has had minimal effect on the broader market. Buyers are advocating for more substantial reductions in instances where the disparity between PP spot and contract prices is relatively large, leading to increased contention among buyers. Producers are striving to maintain PP contract margins despite facing significant challenges. The European petrochemical sector is encountering financial difficulties, resulting in a series of asset closure announcements and ongoing evaluations of numerous assets. Consequently, many PP producers are adopting the position that contract pricing must remain above spot prices to ensure the operational viability of their assets and to guarantee supply security for buyers. The fundamental supply dynamics differ among producers and specific grades, influencing their pricing strategies. China's agreement with the United States to reduce trade tensions through significant tariff cuts by 14 May 2025, for an initial duration of three months, alleviates the pressure on China's substantial propane imports from the US, which are utilized for PP production at their PDH facilities. This development may alleviate concerns regarding China's PP production and could lead to sustained offers from overseas suppliers." Meanwhile in the contract markets, PP injection moulding grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1410-1415/mt FD NWE Germany and FD NWE France levels, both decreased by Euro (-15/mt) week on week. PP injection moulding grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1400-1405/mt FD NWE Italy levels, a drop of Euro (-15/mt) from last week. Meanwhile, PP injection moulding grade prices were assessed at the GBP 1190-1195/mt FD NWE UK levels, a fall of GBP (-10/mt) from the previous week. In the contract markets, PP block copolymer grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1480-1485/mt FD NWE Germany and FD NWE France levels, both dropped by Euro (-15/mt) week on week. PP block copolymer grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1470-1475/mt FD NWE Italy levels, down Euro (-15/mt) from the previous week. Meanwhile, PP block copolymer grade prices were assessed at the GBP 1245-1250/mt FD NWE UK levels, lower by GBP (-15/mt) from last week.In the spot markets, PP injection moulding grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1035-1045/mt FD North West Europe mark while PP block copolymer grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1135-1145/mt FD Northwest Europe levels, both left unchanged week on week. The source further stated, "Spot pricing remained stable throughout the week due to low trading activity, with the market experiencing minimal engagement attributed to a lack of demand for additional spot volumes. Material availability was robust, with no notable supply disruptions reported. Import offers were consistently available during the week from the Middle East and Asia, particularly South Korea. There were mixed reports regarding import pricing, with some indicating that forward delivery prices remained lower than spot prices for immediate delivery, while others found these arbitrages challenging to navigate. Following discussions in Geneva, China and the US announced a 90-day suspension of reciprocal import tariffs. On the operational front, normal supply has not yet resumed from polypropylene (PP) plants in Spain and Portugal, which were taken offline due to an unexpected power outage. The operational status of these facilities remains uncertain, and there have been reports of another shutdown at a site in Spain after a brief restart due to a detected leak in a pipeline supplying naphtha to the cracker. Additionally, a cracker in northern France continues to be offline due to an unrelated power outage that occurred in the latter half of April, although the nearby PP plant has been operational by sourcing propylene through imports. Furthermore, a PP plant in the Netherlands is set to begin a planned turnaround in the last week of May, expected to last until the end of June. In early May, significant logistical challenges resurfaced amidst compounding market difficulties. Despite these supply-side issues, the demand for Polypropylene (PP) continued to decline. Initial weaknesses observed in the construction and automotive sectors have now spread to related industries such as geotextiles, as consumer spending remains constrained, further exacerbating the bearish sentiment in the Polypropylene (PP) market. Buyers have taken a cautious approach, carefully observing the potential impact of the decrease in propylene costs on PP pricing. This has led to a gradual shift in sentiment from cautiousness to anxiety, as market participants await competitively priced PP offers. Looking ahead, European Polypropylene (PP) producers are likely to face ongoing pressure from both weak local demand and competitively priced imports. While quarterly contract arrangements may mitigate sharp price fluctuations in the short term, a further decline in spot Polypropylene (PP) prices is expected in the upcoming weeks. With ample inventories, buyers are anticipated to ramp up negotiations for more advantageous pricing terms, reinforcing the expectation of continued price softness in the European Polypropylene (PP) market." FCA Antwerp PP homopolymer prices were assessed at the Euro 1020-1050/mt level, while FCA Antwerp PP copolymer prices were assessed at the Euro 1120-1150/mt levels, unchanged week on week. Upstream propylene spot prices on Thursday were assessed at the Euro 790-800/mt FD Northwest Europe levels, a decline of Euro (-25/mt) from the previous week. European Propylene contract price for May 2025 settled at the Euro 1015/MT FD North West Europe levels. This price represents a sharp decline of Euro 65/mt from its April 2025 settlement levels.

data.title: Netanyahu accuses France, Britain and Canada of 'emboldening' Hamas
data.description:
Key points:
  • Netanyahu says warnings to Israel play into the hands of Hamas
  • Earlier he said shootings in US underlined threat to Israel
  • Israeli government opposes moves to recognise Palestinian state
  • Western leaders say such recognition does not mean Hamas support

Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu accused the leaders of France, Britain and Canada of wanting to help the Palestinian militant group Hamas after they threatened to take "concrete action" if Israel did not stop its latest offensive in Gaza.

The criticism, echoing similar remarks from Foreign Minister Gideon Saar on Thursday, was part of a fightback by the Israeli government against the increasingly heavy international pressure on it over the war in Gaza.

"You're on the wrong side of humanity and you're on the wrong side of history," Netanyahu said, accusing the three countries of supporting "mass murderers, rapists, baby killers and kidnappers" in reference to the Oct 2023 attacks on Israel.

As the flow of images of destruction and hunger in Gaza has continued, fuelling protests in countries across the world, Israel has struggled to turn world opinion, which has increasingly shifted against it despite the Hamas attacks.

Israeli officials have been particularly concerned about growing calls for European countries including France to follow others such as Spain and Ireland in recognizing a Palestinian state, as part of a two-state solution to resolve decades of conflict in the region.

Netanyahu argues a Palestinian state would threaten Israel and framed the killing of two Israeli embassy staffers in Washington on Tuesday by a man who allegedly shouted "Free Palestine" as a clear example of that threat.

He said "exactly the same chant" was heard during the attack on Israel by Hamas on Oct 7, 2023.

"They don't want a Palestinian state. They want to destroy the Jewish state," he said in a statement on the social media platform X.

"I could never understand how this simple truth evades the leaders of France, Britain, Canada and others," he said, adding that any moves by Western countries to recognize a Palestinian state would "reward these murderers with the ultimate prize."

The Israeli leader, whose government depends on far-right support, said Hamas had thanked French President Emmanuel Macron, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer and Canada's Mark Carney over what he said was their demand for an immediate end to the war.

The leaders' statement on Monday did not demand an immediate end to the war, but a halt to Israel's new military offensive on Gaza and a lifting of its restrictions on humanitarian aid.

Hamas did issue a statement welcoming the move but Netanyahu gave no evidence of any direct contact with the three countries, which all describe the group as a terrorist organisation which should not have any role in running Gaza after the war.

"By issuing their demand – replete with a threat of sanctions against Israel, against Israel, not Hamas – these three leaders effectively said they want Hamas to remain in power," Netanyahu said.

"And they give them hope to establish a second Palestinian state from which Hamas will again seek to destroy the Jewish state."

French Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot said France was "unwaveringly committed to Israel's security".

He said it was determined to combat antisemitism and that it was "absurd and slanderous" to accuse supporters of a two-state solution of encouraging antisemitism or Hamas.

Asked about Netanyahu's remarks, Britain's armed forces minister Luke Pollard said London stood with Israel in their right to self defence, "but that self defence must be conducted within the bounds of international humanitarian law."

"At this moment, we stand fast against terrorism, but we also want to make sure that the aid is getting into Gaza," he told Times Radio.

data.title: Iraq oil ministry: US firms’ direct deals with Kurdistan violate constitution
data.description:
Iraq’s Oil Ministry reaffirmed on Friday its commitment to working with U.S. energy companies but emphasized that cooperation must occur through the federal government and not directly with the Kurdistan Regional Government, which would violate the Iraqi constitution.

Iraq's federal government has reservations about energy agreements signed by the Kurdistan region, after Iraqi Kurdish Prime Minister Masrour Barzani oversaw the signing of two deals worth a combined $110 billion over their lifetimes with U.S. companies.

"Ministry has no objection or reservation regarding engagement with these companies, provided that such engagement is not conducted directly with the Kurdistan Regional Government in isolation from the Federal Government and its official channels, as this would constitute a violation of the Iraqi Constitution and the applicable law", a statement from the oil ministry said on Friday.

"The oil ministry’s statement is a clear message directed at U.S. companies, alerting them to respect Iraq’s valid laws.” an oil ministry official said on Friday.

The ministry added that it welcomes and seeks to work with U.S. companies to develop oil and gas fields.

data.title: Tuniu Gets Nasdaq Noncompliance Notice
data.description:
Tuniu said Friday it is not in compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement for continued listing.

The Chinese online leisure travel company said it has until Nov. 17 to regain compliance by demonstrating that the closing bid price of its American depositary shares is at least $1 each for a minimum of 10 consecutive business days.

Tuniu said it will take "all reasonable measures" to regain compliance.

data.title: JTEKT India Names CFO
data.description:
Auto components manufacturer JTEKT India has appointed Vikas Goel as chief financial officer and wholetime key managerial person, effective from June, according to a Friday filing with the Indian stock exchanges.

He will succeed incumbent CFO Rajiv Chanana, who will be relieved from his current position on May 31 and redesignated as a whole-time director.

The company's shares were down over 2% in recent trade.
